subtopics.title: Reading Comprehension Skills
common_misconceptions: Comprehension is just reading the passage
Answers can always be found word-for-word in the passage
subtopics.teaching_note: Teach students to identify question types (literal, inferential, evaluative). Practice with varied passage types including descriptive, narrative, and argumentative texts.

subtopics.title: Summary Writing Techniques
common_misconceptions: Summary is just copying from the passage
Including as many points as possible makes a better summary
subtopics.teaching_note: Teach the process: read, identify main points, draft in own words, check word count. Emphasize paraphrasing over direct copying.

subtopics.title: Structure and Organization of Essays
common_misconceptions: Longer essays always score higher
Introduction and conclusion are optional
Essays don't need planning
subtopics.teaching_note: Emphasize planning before writing. Teach the hamburger model for paragraph structure. Practice all essay types: narrative, descriptive, argumentative, expository.

subtopics.title: Types of Essays
common_misconceptions: All essays follow the same format
Formal and informal letters have the same tone
subtopics.teaching_note: Teach the features of each essay type. Practice letter formats with proper addresses and salutations. Emphasize audience awareness in speech writing.

subtopics.title: Parts of Speech
common_misconceptions: Every word ending in -ly is an adverb
All verbs show action
subtopics.teaching_note: Use sentence analysis to identify parts of speech. Emphasize that word function determines its part of speech, not just form.

subtopics.title: Tenses and Subject-Verb Agreement
common_misconceptions: Subject-verb agreement only applies to simple tenses
Collective nouns always take plural verbs
subtopics.teaching_note: Teach concord rules systematically. Practice error correction exercises. Focus on tricky cases: collective nouns, indefinite pronouns, and inverted sentences.

subtopics.title: Vowels and Consonants
common_misconceptions: Every letter has only one sound
The number of letters equals the number of sounds
subtopics.teaching_note: Use the International Phonetic Alphabet (IPA). Focus on sounds that differ from Nigerian language phonemes. Practice minimal pairs.

subtopics.title: Rhythm, Stress and Intonation
common_misconceptions: Stressing every word equally in a sentence
Intonation doesn't affect meaning
subtopics.teaching_note: Demonstrate how stress and intonation change meaning. Use recorded materials for listening practice. Practice with question types: WH vs yes/no questions.

subtopics.title: Word Formation Processes
common_misconceptions: Adding any prefix creates the opposite meaning
Synonyms can always be used interchangeably
subtopics.teaching_note: Build vocabulary through word analysis. Teach common prefixes and suffixes with their meanings. Practice identifying word parts.

subtopics.title: Idioms, Proverbs and Figures of Speech
common_misconceptions: Idioms should be interpreted literally
Figurative language is only for literature
subtopics.teaching_note: Teach idioms in context. Compare English idioms with Nigerian language equivalents. Practice identifying figures of speech in everyday language.
